title: Wedelia macrorrhiza
intro: Wedelia macrorrhiza, also known as wild pueraria or elephant's foot, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Wedelia macrorrhiza include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Wedelia macrorrhiza is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal capsules and tinctures for its medicinal benefits.
taxonomy: Wedelia macrorrhiza is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is a member of the Wedelia genus, which consists of various species of flowering plants native to tropical regions. The Wedelia macrorrhiza plant is often classified as a perennial herb, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The species is native to the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in India and Sri Lanka. The plant's classification is also reflected in its botanical name, which includes its genus and specific epithet, indicating its unique characteristics within the Wedelia genus.
